container_template.readme:
# llama.cpp

Serve GGUF models with the official llama.cpp server on SaladCloud GPUs. This container exposes OpenAI-compatible
endpoints for inference and includes the built-in llama.cpp web UI at your deployment URL.

## Changing Models

To switch models, update the environment variables on the container group page:

- **Hugging Face**: set `LLAMA_ARG_HF_REPO` to a GGUF repo (e.g., `bartowski/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct-GGUF`). Use `LLAMA_ARG_HF_FILE` to pick a specific quantization file from the repo.
- **Direct URL**: set `LLAMA_ARG_MODEL_URL` to a direct link to a `.gguf` file.
- **Private/gated models**: set `HF_TOKEN` to your Hugging Face access token.

## Performance Tips

- **GPU Layers** (`LLAMA_ARG_N_GPU_LAYERS`): use `auto` to offload all layers to GPU. Set a specific number if the model doesn't fit in VRAM.
- **Context Size** (`LLAMA_ARG_CTX_SIZE`): larger values use more VRAM. Lower this if you hit out-of-memory errors.
- **Parallel Slots** (`LLAMA_ARG_N_PARALLEL`): number of concurrent requests. More slots = more VRAM usage. Start with 1 and increase if needed.
- **Startup sampling defaults** (`--temp`, `--top-p`, `--min-p`): configured by the recipe via `LLAMA_SERVER_TEMP`, `LLAMA_SERVER_TOP_P`, and `LLAMA_SERVER_MIN_P`.
  These are default startup values and can be overridden per request with `temperature`, `top_p`, and `min_p` in your inference payload.
- **Quantization**: smaller quantizations (Q4_K_M, Q4_K_S) use less VRAM and run faster, with some quality trade-off.

## Resources

- <Link url={`https://${props.networking.dns}`}>Open llama.cpp UI</Link>
- [llama.cpp Server Documentation](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/tree/master/tools/server)
- [CLI Flag to Environment Variable Mapping](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/blob/master/tools/server/README.md#usage)
- [Recipe Source](https://github.com/SaladTechnologies/salad-recipes/tree/master/recipes/llama-cpp)
- <Link url={`https://github.com/SaladTechnologies/salad-recipes/issues/new?title=llama.cpp%20recipe&body=Image%3A%20${props.container.image}`}>Report an Issue on GitHub</Link>

## API endpoints

- `GET /health` - readiness probe and basic health check
- `GET /v1/models` - list available model aliases
- `POST /v1/chat/completions` - OpenAI-compatible chat completions
- `POST /v1/completions` - OpenAI-compatible completions
- `POST /v1/embeddings` - embedding endpoint (model-dependent)

## Curl Example

<Callout variation="note">Omit the `Salad-Api-Key` header if you do not have auth enabled.</Callout>

<CodeBlock language="bash">{`curl https://${props.networking.dns}/v1/chat/completions \\
    -X POST \\
    -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \\
    -H 'Salad-Api-Key: ${props.apiKey}' \\
    -d '{
      "model": "${props.container.environmentVariables.LLAMA_ARG_ALIAS || "llama-cpp"}",
      "messages": [
        {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful assistant."},
        {"role": "user", "content": "Explain how quantization helps GGUF models."}
      ],
      "max_tokens": 128,
      "temperature":0
    }'
`}</CodeBlock>

form.title: llama.cpp
form.description:
Run [llama.cpp](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp) with the official `server-cuda` image on SaladCloud GPUs.

This recipe deploys an OpenAI-compatible API server (`/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/embeddings`, etc.) and configures it through environment variables.

<Callout variation="warning">Ensure your use is permissible under whatever license applies to the model you deploy.</Callout>

## Model Source

Pick one model source for deployment:

- **Hugging Face Repo (GGUF)**: set `LLAMA_ARG_HF_REPO` (and optionally `LLAMA_ARG_HF_FILE`).
- **Direct Model URL**: set `LLAMA_ARG_MODEL_URL`.

### Example Models

Hugging Face repo examples (`LLAMA_ARG_HF_REPO`):

- `ggml-org/gemma-3-1b-it-GGUF`
- `bartowski/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-GGUF`
- `unsloth/Qwen3-Coder-Next-GGUF:UD-Q4_K_XL`

Direct URL examples (`LLAMA_ARG_MODEL_URL`):

- `https://huggingface.co/ggml-org/gemma-3-1b-it-GGUF/resolve/main/gemma-3-1b-it-Q4_K_M.gguf`
- `https://huggingface.co/bartowski/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-GGUF/resolve/main/Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct-Q4_K_M.gguf`

## GPU and Runtime Controls

This form sets common settings like:

- `LLAMA_ARG_N_GPU_LAYERS`
- `LLAMA_ARG_CTX_SIZE`
- `LLAMA_ARG_N_PARALLEL`
- `LLAMA_ARG_ALIAS`
- startup `--temp`, `--top-p`, `--min-p` defaults

<Callout variation="info">
`--temp`, `--top-p`, and `--min-p` are wired as startup command args in this recipe because they are not currently mapped to `LLAMA_ARG_*` environment variables. These are default values and can still be overridden per request with `temperature`, `top_p`, and `min_p` in inference payloads.
</Callout>

For full control, use **Advanced Configuration** and add any supported `LLAMA_ARG_*` variable from the llama.cpp server docs:

- [Server Args + Env Vars](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/blob/master/tools/server/README.md#usage)
- [Official Git Repo](https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/tree/master/tools/server)

Use `HF_TOKEN` only if your chosen Hugging Face model is private or gated.

container_group_name.title: Container Group Name
container_group_name.description: Required* Must be 2-63 lowercase letters, numbers, or hyphens. Cannot start with a number or start or end with a hyphen.

model_source.title: Model Source
model_source.description: Required* Choose where llama.cpp should load the GGUF model from.

hf_token.title: Hugging Face Token
hf_token.description: Optional. Required only for private or gated Hugging Face models.

n_gpu_layers.title: GPU Layers
n_gpu_layers.description: Number of layers to offload to GPU (`LLAMA_ARG_N_GPU_LAYERS`). Use `auto`, `all`, or an integer.

alias.title: Model Alias
alias.description: Model name exposed by the API (`LLAMA_ARG_ALIAS`).

sampling_temp.title: Startup Temperature
sampling_temp.description: Default `--temp` value passed as a startup command arg.

sampling_top_p.title: Startup Top-p
sampling_top_p.description: Default `--top-p` value passed as a startup command arg.

sampling_min_p.title: Startup Min-p
sampling_min_p.description: Default `--min-p` value passed as a startup command arg.

networking_auth.title: Require Container Gateway Authentication
networking_auth.description: When enabled, requests must include a SaladCloud API Key. When disabled, public (unauthenticated) access is allowed.

model_hf_repo.title: Hugging Face GGUF Repo
model_hf_repo.description: Required* Hugging Face repo in `<org>/<repo>[:quant]` format for `LLAMA_ARG_HF_REPO`.

model_hf_file.title: Hugging Face File (Optional)
model_hf_file.description: Optional `LLAMA_ARG_HF_FILE` override if you want a specific file inside the repo.

model_url.title: Model URL
model_url.description: Required* Direct URL to a `.gguf` file for `LLAMA_ARG_MODEL_URL`.

short_description: Serve GGUF models with llama.cpp on SaladCloud GPUs.
